name: Dullahan
primary_sources: Croker's Fairy Legends and Traditions of the South of Ireland

distinctive_features: head with ashy pale skin and fiery eyes
mouth with razor-sharp teeth forming a wide grin
drives a horse-drawn carriage made of human bones
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: human-sized rider on a large horse
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: head carried in hand or under arm, seated on horse or driving a carriage

domain_specific_functional.sight_ability: uses severed head to scan for mortals about to die
domain_specific_functional.whip: uses a human spine as a whip

relationships.description: The Dullahan is the driver of the Headless Coach, a harbinger of death.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbolizes death and misfortune in Irish folklore
primary_sources: Croker's Fairy Legends and Traditions of the South of Ireland

contrastive_constraints: NOT culturally detached from Irish folklore
